Port hide add/remove operations require the target port; list and clear modes can omit it.
Token copy requires the source process ID; system and restore modes use driver-managed token state.
Global hook installation requires the target module, target function, and replacement address; query mode can omit hook targets.
Global hook removal requires the hook slot index; query mode can omit it.
Infinity hook enable/disable operations require the target syscall number; query mode can omit it.
Infinity hook enable requires the replacement handler address.
PTE write/restore operations require the replacement PTE value; read and make_writable modes derive values from the current PTE.
MSR writes require the target MSR index and replacement value; reads may use the handler default index.
Driver cloak target mode requires the driver module name; self and query modes can omit it.
System thread creation requires a kernel start routine address; query mode can omit it.
Kernel exec run/alloc operations require shellcode bytes; free mode requires an existing allocation address.
Kernel exec free requires the allocated kernel address returned by a prior allocation.
PPL strip/set operations require the target process ID; query mode can omit it only when the handler default is intended.
Control register writes require the target register index and replacement value; reads may default to CR0.
IDT writes require the interrupt vector and replacement handler address; read and dump modes may use defaults.
Unloaded-driver clear_name requires the driver module name; query and clear_all modes can omit it.
Token steal/swap operations require the target process ID; query mode can omit it only when explicitly selected.
Process protection set/strip operations require the target process ID; query mode can omit it only when explicitly selected.
Registry hide add/remove operations require the target registry key path; list and clear modes can omit it.
File lock add/remove operations require the target file path; list and clear modes can omit it.
ETW provider disable/enable operations require the provider GUID; query and kill_all modes can omit it.
EPROCESS image-name spoofing requires the target process ID and new image name.
EPROCESS command-line spoofing requires the target process ID and new command line.
EPROCESS parent-PID spoofing requires the target process ID and new parent PID.
Credential memory reads require the source process ID and address; find_lsass and full dump modes derive their target internally.
Driver impersonation swap requires both target and legitimate driver paths; restore/query use stored backup state.
Callback single-remove requires the callback table index; enum, nuke_all, and restore modes do not use it.
Minifilter detach requires the filter name and frame ID; enum and nuke modes can omit a specific target.
Kernel APC shellcode injection requires the target thread ID, shellcode size, and shellcode address.
Kernel APC DLL injection requires the target thread ID and DLL path.
WFP single-remove requires the target callout ID; enum and nuke modes can omit a single callout target.

access_mask.description: Access mask to strip (0 = close handle)

action.description: Kernel action. Groups: read-only status/readiness, generic helpers (driver_load/read/pte_modify/etc), hybrid actions (ppl_bypass/dkom_hide/token_escalate use memoric.sys unless device_path is provided), and direct memoric.sys actions (driver_*). Prefer canonical driver_* names over legacy aliases like notify_routine/reg_protect/object_hook/port_hide.

address.description: Kernel physical/virtual address. Integer or hex string like '0xFFFFF80000000000'.

alloc_address.description: Address of previously allocated kernel pool

allow_protected_target.description: Explicit override for state-changing operations against protected or critical Windows targets

allowed_pid.description: PID exempted from file lock restrictions

array_address.description: Kernel callback array address for callback enumeration/removal helpers

artifact_retention_secs.description: Retention window for process-local artifact resource links emitted by this call; values outside the registry bounds are rejected before dispatch

as_task.description: Compatibility flag for task-augmented execution: run eligible read-only or dry-run calls in the process-local MCP task registry for polling/cancellation. MCP clients may prefer params.task.

base_address.description: Base address for driver_pe_dump/driver_process_dump (hex string or integer; 0 = auto/full range)

bytes.description: Bytes to write (canonical for kernel write / physical_write)

callback_address.description: Callback address for verification/removal (hex string or integer)

callback_list_offset.description: Optional OBJECT_TYPE.CallbackList offset override for object_callback_enum

cr3.description: Target process CR3 for pte_modify BYOVD page table walks

data.description: Legacy alias for bytes on kernel(action='write')

device_path.description: Explicit BYOVD device path (e.g. \\.\RTCore64). If present, hybrid actions use BYOVD instead of memoric.sys.

driver_name.description: Driver module name for hiding (e.g. memoric.sys)

dry_run.description: Preview a state-changing operation without executing it where supported

entry_address.description: Kernel callback entry address returned by callback enumeration helpers

flags.description: Generic driver flags field used by driver_process_dump and similar actions

frame_id.description: Filter manager frame ID for driver_minifilter_detach(detach)

handler_address.description: Custom handler address for infinity hook

input_struct.description: Raw input buffer bytes for custom BYOVD IOCTL layouts

ioctl_code.description: Explicit IOCTL code for kernel read/write device operations

ioctl_read_code.description: BYOVD read IOCTL code for legacy callback enumeration helpers

ioctl_write_code.description: BYOVD write IOCTL code for legacy callback removal helpers

list_head_address.description: Optional CmpCallBackListHead address override for registry_callback_enum

log_name.description: Optional event log name for driver_event_log_clear

max_dump_size.description: Max PE dump size in bytes

max_events.description: Max events to return from ring buffer

max_keys.description: Maximum key events to read for driver_keylogger

new_dpl.description: New descriptor privilege level for driver_idt_rw(write)

object_type_address.description: Optional OBJECT_TYPE pointer address override for object_callback_enum

output_path.description: Optional artifact output path for driver_pe_dump, driver_process_dump, and other large kernel dump results

pool_tag.description: Kernel pool tag filter for driver_memory_pool. Integer raw tag or 4-char ASCII string like 'Proc'.

protect_pid.description: PID to protect via object callback

read_ioctl.description: BYOVD read IOCTL code for pte_modify/vad_hide or hybrid kernel helpers

redaction.description: Result redaction profile. strict suppresses raw bytes, hex blobs, credential-like fields, and paths from inline output.

replacement_addr.description: Replacement function address for global hook

request_id.description: Optional caller request ID for audit/provenance

shellcode_address.description: VA of mapped shellcode in target (hex string or integer)

source_pid.description: Source PID for token duplication (0 = System)

strip_access.description: Access bits to strip from handle opens

target_function.description: Function name for global hook (e.g. NtQuerySystemInformation)

target_module.description: Kernel module name for global hook target (e.g. ntoskrnl.exe)

target_pid.description: Target PID for token swap or other driver target actions

task_id.description: Task ID propagated to long-running handlers for cooperative cancellation

thread_start.description: Kernel address for system thread start routine

tid.description: Thread ID for driver_kernel_apc legacy path

timeout_ms.description: Per-call cooperative timeout in milliseconds; long-running handlers check it at safe boundaries

value.description: Generic integer value used by driver_cr_rw and similar actions

write_ioctl.description: BYOVD write IOCTL code for pte_modify/vad_hide or hybrid kernel helpers
